workItem.title: Reconcile terminal Issues with durable AgentRun projections
workItem.body:

## Context

A live recovery inspection found closed bos-mcp Issues 8 and 9 whose GitHub audit run manifests still report status running. The scheduler process inventory was correct, but repository-first reconstruction cannot treat a stale run projection as live state.

## Expected outcome

Symphony and the BOS Delivery lifecycle idempotently finalize or reconcile AgentRuns when their canonical Issue or merged PR is terminal, without recreating runs, attempts, comments, claims, evidence, approvals, or pull requests.

## Scope

- Detect closed Issues and merged pull requests whose AgentRun projection is non-terminal
- Persist an explicit reconciliation AuditEvent and terminal run projection
- Release or supersede expired claims without erasing history
- Run reconciliation on startup and periodic recovery polling
- Keep the live process inventory distinct from historical ledger projections
- Add restart, lost-response, duplicate-operation and already-terminal tests

## Out of scope

- Rewrite or delete existing audit events
- Infer successful delivery when evidence or merge state is absent
- Access GitHub outside game-api

## Acceptance criteria

- A closed Issue with a stale running run is reconciled to the correct terminal state exactly once
- A merged PR and exact merge commit remain linked to the finalized run
- An open or genuinely active run is never finalized by reconciliation
- A lost response is recovered using the same operation identity without duplicate events
- A clean-host recovery does not list closed historical work as an active run
- The full Symphony gate passes
